title: Noah Kahan — The Great Divide Tour
location: Truist Park, 755 Battery Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Big-stadium folk-pop night at The Battery TONIGHT — pair with a Battery pre-dinner stroll for a full Monday date.

title: R.A.S World Cup Atlanta — 100% Reggae · Afrobeat · Soca
location: Air Rooftop Bar ATL, Atlanta, GA
description: Midweek Caribbean rooftop — reggae, Afrobeats and soca under one DJ rotation. Late-night date that turns into a 4th-of-July-weekend warm-up.

title: HELIUM COMEDY: Mo Amer
location: Helium Comedy Club, 10 North Hwy N, Alpharetta, GA
description: Mo Amer (Netflix/Mo) — storytelling-style stand-up with global bite. Great 'let me pick something funny for us' date.

title: Paula Poundstone at Buckhead Theatre
location: Buckhead Theatre, 3110 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA
price: Ticketed (Live Nation; upgrades for lounge/bottomless popcorn)
description: Living-legend observational comedian at a sit-down Buckhead theatre. Date-night classic: dinner in Buckhead, then laugh for 90 minutes.

title: Shakira — Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour (World Cup Edition)
location: State Farm Arena, 1 State Farm Dr, Atlanta, GA
description: Full arena spectacle with a Latin-pop setlist that demands a sing-along date. Pair with a downtown dinner first, walk to the arena.

title: AFROXFEST — The African Experience Festival
location: Candler Park, Atlanta, GA
description: All-day Afrobeats + pan-African culture festival in Candler Park. Wear something bright, graze the food stalls, dance between sets.

title: Dinner and A Date: A Night of Soul — Live Music Show
location: Apache XLR, Atlanta, GA
description: Date-night-themed soul show — built-for-couples Sunday with dinner + a live band. Easy win if you're not sure what to plan.

title: SOUL SUNDAYS on the Rooftop at REVERB
location: Reverb by Hard Rock Downtown Atlanta, Atlanta, GA
description: Rooftop soul + R&B Sunday with skyline views. Golden-hour to dark-transition date — sunset, cocktails, slow songs.

title: Bamba Afrobeats & Kompa Day Party
location: Atlanta, GA (venue on AllEvents)
description: Sunday Afrobeats + Haitian Kompa day party — daylight dancefloor before the workweek. Couples who want movement without the late-night hangover.

title: Yon Re El & Friends Play Herbie! — Jazz/Funk/Soul Tribute
location: 599 N Central Ave, Hapeville, GA
description: Herbie Hancock tribute in a small Hapeville room — jazz heads + funk + soul. Date that ends with 'I didn't know Atlanta had this.'

title: Vinyl Sessions: June Edition — Listening Night
location: Marcus Bar & Grille, Atlanta, GA
description: Curated vinyl-DJ listening night — quiet enough to actually talk, musical enough to feel like a 'thing.' Built for a real-conversation date.

title: Latin Wednesdays at Tongue & Groove
location: Tongue & Groove, Atlanta, GA
description: Weekly Latin social — salsa, bachata, reggaetón under the Buckhead lights. Built for couples who want a midweek dance date.

title: SOLARA: Return to the Light — 404 Found ATL
location: Binders Art Supplies and Frames, Buckhead, Atlanta, GA
description: Afternoon art-marker opening in Buckhead — easy to walk, look, and turn into dinner + drinks. Built for a 'first real date' that doesn't try too hard.

title: Afrobeats to the World — Atlanta
location: Atlanta, GA (venue on ATTW site)
description: 18+ Afrobeats party night — DJs, dancefloor, international crowd. Late, loud, and exactly the 'wear something cute, lose your voice' kind of Friday.

title: First Friday at First "LIVE" — Rod Harris, Jr.
location: First "LIVE" venue, Atlanta, GA (see Eventbrite)
description: Intimate live-jazz series kickoff — sax-led trio in a small room. The kind of Friday that starts with wine and ends with a new favorite song.

title: Afrobeats to the World (18+)
location: The Masquerade – Heaven, 75 MLK Jr Dr SW, Atlanta, GA (Kenny's Alley, Underground Atlanta)
description: High-energy Afrobeats night with DJs and a packed dance floor. 18+ crowd, indoor venue, last batch of $12 tix — a perfect way to kick off the weekend together. Expect nonstop hits from across the diaspora and a crowd that came to move.

title: Rum Punch Fest — Reggae on the Rooftop (4th of July Weekend)
location: Cafe Circa, Atlanta, GA
description: Independence Day reggae rooftop with rum punch and skyline views. Fireworks + Caribbean food + dancing = the loudest, easiest 4th-of-July date in town.

title: Rooftop Rodeo — July 4th at Ponce City Market
location: The Roof at Ponce City Market, 675 Ponce de Leon Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: America's 250th birthday bash + 10 years of The Roof. Live DJs, line dancing, mechanical bull, contests, food and drinks with skyline views. A whole-afternoon-into-night date in one place — dress cute, ride the slide, watch fireworks from up high.

title: Rum Punch Fest: Reggae on the Rooftop (July 4th Weekend)
location: Cafe Circa Rooftop, Atlanta, GA
description: Late-night rooftop reggae party for 4th of July weekend with rum punch specials, DJs, and skyline views. Bring a partner who can hang past midnight — dancing, cocktails, and that built-in holiday-weekend energy.

title: Michael Blackson (Comedy)
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: City Winery + KISS 104.1 present Michael Blackson — a holiday-weekend date with a proper dinner-and-a-show setup. Wine, food, and one of the most recognizable names in Black comedy. Pair it with a pre-show dinner in the venue.

title: Reverb & Vibe: Rooftop Salsa at Sunset
location: Reverb Hotel Atlanta, Downtown
description: Sunset rooftop salsa social hosted by Mambo Mayhem — great for beginners and experienced dancers alike. Rooftop golden hour, Latin music, and easy instruction make this a low-pressure first-dance-together date.

title: I LOVE MUSIC meets BROWN SUGAR (Guardian Works)
location: Guardian Works, Atlanta, GA
description: A feel-good afternoon-into-evening music event with DJs, vendors, and a community vibe. Casual Sunday date — wander the booths together, grab food, and dance when the right song hits.

title: Vegan Soul Sunday — Spice & Herbs for Healing
location: New Life Presbyterian Church, Atlanta, GA
description: Plant-based pop-up celebrating vegan soul food with an herbs & healing theme. A chill Sunday afternoon date — sample dishes together, support local vegan chefs, and walk away full and inspired.

title: Day 7 — Killer Mike, Tap Rebels, Africa x Tech & More (Cultural Experience)
location: The Center (CTR) Atlanta, Atlanta, GA
description: Atlanta Cultural Experience day party with Killer Mike, Tap Rebels, and Africa x Tech on the lineup. All-day outdoor music, food, and Black culture — a Monday date that turns into a whole vibe. Bring comfortable shoes and your dancing energy.

title: Summer Slamajama — Gallery Opening
location: Anne Irwin Fine Art, Atlanta, GA
description: Summer group show opening with art, music, and mingling. Easy Thursday-evening gallery date — meet the artists, share wine, and end with dinner in Buckhead.

title: Na'im Lynn (Comedy Special Event)
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
price: Couple's Package: 2 premium seats + $90 F&B credit (see Helium)
description: Kevin Hart's longtime opener and BET/Foxxhole alum Na'im Lynn headlines Helium. The Couple's Package is built for exactly this: reserved seats, food credit, and a built-in two-item minimum. Easy date — laugh together, eat together, drive home together.

title: Atlanta Summer Halal Food Festival (2 Days, BeltLine)
location: Historic Fourth Ward Park / Old Fourth Ward Skatepark (Atlanta BeltLine)
price: See Atlanta Muslim Festival Collective (low-cost entry)
description: 80+ halal food vendors, drinks, crafts, and culture along the BeltLine. Perfect weekend date — graze your way through Atlanta's halal scene, walk it off on the trail, and pick a winner for dinner.

title: Rum Island Fest (Caribbean Music Festival)
location: Piedmont Park, Atlanta, GA
description: Inaugural Caribbean culture festival headlined by dancehall star Aidonia and rising artist Ayetian, plus Soca, Afrobeats, Reggaeton, DJ sets, rum tastings, and a Caribbean food village. Big outdoor date energy — bring a blanket, share plates, dance in the grass.

title: Afro Caribbean Fest Atlanta (Day Party)
location: Embr Lounge, Atlanta, GA
description: Indoor + outdoor daytime party spinning '90s/'00s reggae, Afrobeats, and R&B. Free entry early, cash bar, and a mixed crowd that loves to dance. Easy, low-cost date that turns into a full night out.

title: Jill Scott — To Whom This May Concern Tour (with Moonchild)
location: Fox Theatre, 660 Peachtree St NE, Midtown Atlanta
price: Varies (premier R&B tour — on sale now)
description: Jill Scott at the historic Fox Theatre — neo-soul royalty in a venue built for date nights. Get dressed up, share a drink in the Fox's lobby, and sing along to a catalog full of love songs. One of the marquee couple's nights of the month.

title: NE-YO & AKON — Nights Like This Tour 2026
location: Cellairis Amphitheatre at Lakewood, Atlanta, GA
description: R&B/Afropop double-bill under the summer sky at Lakewood. Lawn or seats, food trucks, and sing-along hits. Great 'big summer show' date — bring a blanket and turn it into a whole evening.

title: Harry Connick Jr. (Live)
location: Synovus Bank Amphitheater at Chastain Park, Atlanta, GA
description: Harry Connick Jr. at one of Atlanta's most romantic outdoor venues — Chastain at night with picnic tables and a string-lit lawn. Bring a bottle of wine, a picnic, and a date who likes Sinatra-era swagger.

title: Saxophonist Jessy J (Dinner & a Show)
location: The Perfect Note Atlanta, Duluth, GA
description: Contemporary jazz saxophonist Jessy J in an intimate dinner-and-a-show setting. Pair dinner with smooth jazz for a classic two-for-one date night. Worth the short drive to Duluth for the vibe.

title: Vinnie D. & Co. — Better Get It in Your Soul (Soul Jazz)
location: Red Light Cafe, Atlanta, GA
description: A soul-jazz night in one of Atlanta's coziest listening rooms. Dim lights, great music, and a small-room vibe that practically forces good conversation between sets. Ideal for couples who like jazz and want something more intimate than a big arena show.

title: Prima Materia — Group Exhibition Opening
location: Empire Arts Gallery, Atlanta, GA
description: Group show exploring the 'sacred beginning' of the creative act. A perfect Saturday-afternoon art date — slow walk, talk about what you see, then grab coffee or dinner nearby in the gallery district.

title: ATL Vegan Festival
location: Piedmont Park, 1332 Monroe Dr NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Atlanta's biggest plant-based festival — dozens of vegan food vendors, cooking demos, and live music in the park. Picnic-blanket date energy with the best of Atlanta's vegan scene in one place. Sunny, casual, and easy.

title: Tease Tuesday Burlesque
location: Red Light Café, Atlanta, GA
description: Atlanta's long-running burlesque night — playful, theatrical, and surprisingly romantic. Pair it with cocktails and a pre-show dinner on the Eastside for a date night that's a little spicier than the usual movie-and-dinner.

title: Karlous Miller (Special Event)
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
description: Wild 'N Out / 85 South favorite Karlous Miller brings high-energy crowd work to Helium. Comedy-club dates are gold: laugh together, grab a drink between sets, walk out with a story. Two nights — pick one, or both.

title: Savanna Nights at Zoo Atlanta (Taylor Swift & Friends)
location: Zoo Atlanta, 800 Cherokee Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: After-hours adults-only zoo night themed around Taylor Swift & friends. Walk the zoo with cocktails, listen to live music at spots throughout the park, and lean into the most date-friendly thing Atlanta does all summer.

title: Lionel Richie & Earth, Wind & Fire — Sing A Song All Night Long
location: State Farm Arena, Atlanta, GA
description: Two legends, one night — pure sing-along R&B and funk. This is a 'dress up, get the seats together, sing every word' kind of date. Hard to think of a more crowd-pleasing couple's show this month.

title: Daniel Caesar — Son of Spergy Tour
location: State Farm Arena, Atlanta, GA
description: Daniel Caesar's intimate, romantic R&B in an arena setting — 'Best Part,' 'Get You,' and a catalog built for slow dancing. A 'lean-into-each-other' kind of show, perfect for the couple that likes modern soul.

title: Buju Banton & Stephen Marley
location: Cellairis Amphitheatre at Lakewood, Atlanta, GA
description: Reggae royalty under the stars — Buju Banton and Stephen Marley, two generations of roots reggae. Outdoor amphitheatre, picnic energy, and a sound that turns a regular Saturday into a memory.
